name: golang-github-jackc-pgmock-dev
title: golang-github-jackc-pgmock-dev
summary: provides the ability to mock a PostgreSQL server
description:
provides the ability to mock a PostgreSQL server
pgmock provides the ability to mock a PostgreSQL server.
.
See pgmock_test.go for example usage.
.
pgmockproxy is a PostgreSQL proxy that logs the messages back and forth
between the PostgreSQL client and server. This can aid in building a mocking
script by running commands against a real server to observe the results. It
can also be used to debug applications that speak the PostgreSQL wire
protocol without needing to use a tool like Wireshark.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use golang-github-jackc-pgmock-dev because provides the ability to mock a PostgreSQL server.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use golang-github-jackc-pgmock-dev as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair golang-github-jackc-pgmock-dev with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `golang-github-jackc-pgmock-dev --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
